STIPULATION FOR HEARING BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM BRAD KAPLAN FILED BY DATWANI, AKASH BOYD, JR, BENTON SURRELL
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUN-05-2009 - JUDGMENT. (CLERK-BETH WILBER, ROOM 103)

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF DATWANI, AKASH RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT BOYD, JR, BENTON SURRELL $3,466.35 PRINCIPAL, $80.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $3,546.35, * $1750.00 OF THIS AWARD REPRESENTS STATUTORY PENALTIES FOR DEFENDANT'S BAD FAITH RETENTION OF A SECURITY DEPOSIT PER CIVIL CODE 1950.5. ADDITIONALLY, NO DEDUCTION FROM THE SECURITY DEPOSIT WAS MADE FOR THE "AUTOMATIC DEDUCTION" AS A CLEANING FEE, AS SUCH FEE CONSTITUTES A NON-REFUNDABLE PORTION OF A SECURITY DEPOSIT IN VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 1950.5(M). STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS NO EXHIBITS FILED
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-20-2009 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 525

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Sep-23-2009 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 10:45am (related cases 829205 & 829206 are heard together)
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal, Judge Pro Tem Richard Beckman, nothing on record, clerk-Beth Wilber, Dept. 525
Appellant, Benton Surrell Boyd, appears through counsel, Zanghi Torres Arshawsky, LLP; Respondent, Akash Datwani, appears in Pro Per. Evidence from the small claims hearing is returned to parties to use in presenting their respective cases. Parties, previously sworn, present their respective cases in the narrative.
EXHR(A) Packet of evidence
EXHA(1) Packet of evidence
EXHA(2) Sample piece of flooring
11:30am
The Court asks Appellant's and Respondent's to each get a new estimate on refinishing the flooring, and give them to Mr. Arshawsky. Mr. Arshawasky will then give the documents to Judge Beckman, who will then rule. New evidence is retained, matter is submitted.

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Judgment Entered
